claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for UK Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child (or children) you're claiming for:**

How old is the child you want to claim Child Benefit for?

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

Please let me know the child's age, and if you're claiming for multiple children, please tell me about each of them.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! Thanks for helping me with this. I have two children I'd like to claim for - Alex is 5 years old and Blake is 10 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you for providing those details! Since both Alex (5 years old) and Blake (10 years old) are under 16, they both automatically qualify for the age requirement.

Let me continue with the next step in the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check about the children's work status:

**Are both Alex and Blake working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work at all)?**

Given their ages (5 and 10), I imagine neither of them is working, but I just need to confirm this to continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh no, neither of them work at all! They're just kids - Alex is only 5 and Blake is 10. They're both in school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Of course, that makes perfect sense! Since neither Alex nor Blake is working, they both meet this requirement.

Let me continue to the next step.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question: Are Alex and Blake NOT receiving any benefits in their own right?**

This means they are not personally claiming benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit in their own name.

Given their ages, I'd expect they aren't receiving such benefits, but I just need to confirm this.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them receives any benefits. They're just regular kids - they don't have anything like that in their own names.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming! Neither Alex nor Blake receives benefits in their own right, so they both pass this check too.

Let me move to the next step.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question: Are Alex and Blake in any apprenticeships?**

Since they're 5 and 10 years old and in school, I'd assume they're not in apprenticeships. Just to confirm - neither Alex nor Blake is currently enrolled in any apprenticeship programme, correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, of course not! They're way too young for apprenticeships. Alex is only 5 and Blake is 10 - they're both just in regular school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Absolutely, you're right! At 5 and 10, they're definitely too young for apprenticeships. Both Alex and Blake pass this check as well.

Let me continue to the next step.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check about your residency status.

**Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You can qualify through **any one** of the following:

1. **Normally live in the UK** - You're a resident in the UK
2. **Settled status** - You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **Pre-settled status and working** - You have pre-settled status and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold for 3+ continuous months)
4. **Pre-settled status and seeking work** - You have pre-settled status and are actively looking for work
5. **Pre-settled status with sufficient resources** - You have pre-settled status and can support yourself financially (without claiming certain benefits)
6. **Pre-settled status and studying** - You have pre-settled status and are studying with sufficient resources
7. **Pre-settled status as a family member** - You have pre-settled status as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national
8. **Crown servant abroad** - You're living abroad as a Crown servant

Do any of these apply to you? For most people, option 1 (normally living in the UK) is the most common.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Actually, I don't live in the UK. I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you're living abroad, I need to check if you might still qualify through one specific path.

**Are you living abroad as a Crown servant?**

Crown servants include people working for the UK government overseas, such as:
- Diplomats
- Members of the armed forces stationed abroad
- Civil servants posted overseas

Do you fall into this category, or are you living abroad for other reasons?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant. I'm just living abroad for other reasons.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. Unfortunately, since you are living abroad and are not a Crown servant, you do not meet the UK residency requirements for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'm sorry to deliver this news, but based on the assessment, I've reached a conclusion about your Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Assessment Result: **Not Eligible**

I'm sorry, but based on the information you've provided, **you are not currently eligible for Child Benefit** for either Alex or Blake.

### Summary

| Child | Age | Eligible? | Reason |
|-------|-----|-----------|--------|
| Alex | 5 | ❌ No | Claimant doesn't meet residency requirements |
| Blake | 10 | ❌ No | Claimant doesn't meet residency requirements |

### Why?

Both Alex and Blake fully qualify as eligible children – they're under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, and not in apprenticeships. However, **Child Benefit requires the claimant (you) to meet UK residency requirements**, and since you're living abroad and are not a Crown servant, you don't currently meet these requirements.

### What You Can Do

Your circumstances may change in the future. You **could become eligible** if:

- You return to live in the UK
- You gain settled status or pre-settled status (with qualifying criteria) under the EU Settlement Scheme
- You become employed as a Crown servant abroad

If your situation changes, I'd encourage you to reassess your eligibility at that time.

For more information, you can visit: https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit eligibility?

reasonings: Alex (age 5) meets all the child-related eligibility criteria: under 16 years old, not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant (parent) does not meet the UK residency requirements as they are living abroad and are not a Crown servant. Therefore, Child Benefit cannot be claimed for Alex.

Blake (age 10) meets all the child-related eligibility criteria: under 16 years old, not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant (parent) does not meet the UK residency requirements as they are living abroad and are not a Crown servant. Therefore, Child Benefit cannot be claimed for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: While both children (Alex, age 5, and Blake, age 10) fully meet the eligibility criteria as qualifying children, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit because they do not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad and is not a Crown servant, which means they do not satisfy any of the residency pathways required for Child Benefit eligibility.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.

Blake (age 10) meets all the child-related eligibility criteria: under 16 years old, not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant (parent) does not meet the UK residency requirements as they are living abroad and are not a Crown servant. Therefore, Child Benefit cannot be claimed for Blake.
